name: ClientDataManager
description: Main data management class that wraps react-query's QueryClient. Provides tag-based cache invalidation, source-level invalidation, and reset methods. Used as the central data layer for Gravity UI applications.
when_to_use: Setting up the application-wide data management layer
Invalidating cached queries by tag after a mutation
Resetting or clearing specific data sources
avoid: Direct react-query QueryClient usage when data-source patterns are in use — use ClientDataManager methods instead

parameters.name: config.defaultOptions
parameters.description: Default react-query options for all queries (staleTime, retry, etc).

examples: const dataManager = new ClientDataManager({ defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: 300000, retry: 3 } } });

name: DataInfiniteLoader
description: React component that handles loading, error, and pagination states for infinite queries. Renders LoadingView during initial load, ErrorView on failure, and MoreView for pagination controls.
when_to_use: Displaying paginated or infinite-scroll data with loading and error handling
Wrapping content that depends on an infinite query data source

props.name: error
props.description: Error object from the query, or null.

props.name: hasNextPage
props.description: Whether more pages are available to load.

props.name: fetchNextPage
props.description: Function to trigger loading the next page.

props.name: isFetchingNextPage
props.description: Whether the next page is currently being fetched.

props.name: MoreView
props.description: Component shown for the 'load more' control.

props.name: children
props.description: Content to render when data is available.

examples:
<DataInfiniteLoader
  status={status}
  error={error}
  hasNextPage={hasNextPage}
  fetchNextPage={fetchNextPage}
  isFetchingNextPage={isFetchingNextPage}
  LoadingView={Spinner}
  ErrorView={ErrorMessage}
  MoreView={LoadMoreButton}
>
  {data.map((item) => <Item key={item.id} data={item} />)}
</DataInfiniteLoader>

name: DataLoader
description: React component that handles loading and error states for plain queries. Shows LoadingView while fetching and ErrorView on failure, otherwise renders children.
when_to_use: Wrapping content that depends on a data source query
Displaying a spinner during data fetch and an error message on failure
avoid: Paginated or infinite-scroll data — use DataInfiniteLoader instead

props.name: children
props.description: Content to render when data is loaded.

examples:
<DataLoader status={status} error={error} errorAction={refetch} LoadingView={Spinner} ErrorView={ErrorMessage}>
  {data && <UserCard user={data} />}
</DataLoader>

name: DataManagerProvider
description: React context provider that makes a DataManager instance available to all child components via the DataManagerContext.
when_to_use: Wrapping the application root to provide DataManager to all descendant components
avoid: Providing multiple DataManagers in nested trees — use a single root provider

name: idle
description: Special symbol that disables query execution when passed as params to useQueryData. The query remains in 'success' status with undefined data, maintaining type safety for conditional queries.
when_to_use: Conditionally skipping a query when required parameters are not yet available
Replacing enabled: false for type-safe conditional queries
avoid: When you need the query to actively cancel — idle prevents execution but doesn't cancel in-flight requests
examples: const { data } = useQueryData(userDataSource, userId ? { userId } : idle);
// Query won't fire until condition is met, status stays 'success'

name: makeInfiniteQueryDataSource
description: Creates an infinite query data source definition for pagination and infinite scroll patterns. Wraps react-query's infinite query with data-source conventions including tag-based cache invalidation.
when_to_use: Creating a data source for paginated list APIs
Implementing infinite scroll with automatic next-page detection
avoid: Simple single-fetch data — use makePlainQueryDataSource instead

parameters.name: config.name
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the data source.

parameters.name: config.fetch
parameters.description: Fetch function; use skipContext() to adapt existing API functions.

parameters.name: config.next
parameters.description: Returns params for the next page, or undefined to stop.

parameters.name: config.prev
parameters.description: Returns params for the previous page.

parameters.name: config.tags
parameters.description: Tag generator for cache invalidation.

name: makePlainQueryDataSource
description: Creates a plain (single-fetch) query data source definition. Supports parameter and response transformation, tag-based cache invalidation, and react-query options.
when_to_use: Defining a typed data source for a single API endpoint
Adding tag-based cache invalidation to a data fetch
avoid: Paginated or infinite-scroll data — use makeInfiniteQueryDataSource instead

parameters.name: config.name
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the data source.

parameters.name: config.fetch
parameters.description: Fetch function; use skipContext() to adapt existing API functions.

parameters.name: config.transformParams
parameters.description: Transform input params before the request.

parameters.name: config.transformResponse
parameters.description: Transform response data.

parameters.name: config.tags
parameters.description: Generate tags for cache invalidation.

parameters.name: config.options
parameters.description: React Query options (staleTime, retry, etc).

examples: const { data, status, error, refetch } = useQueryData(userDataSource, { userId: 123 });

name: skipContext
description: Utility function that wraps an existing fetch function to skip the context and fetchContext arguments automatically prepended by data-source. Allows using plain API functions without modification.
when_to_use: Adapting existing API functions to work with data-source's fetch interface
avoid: When the function already handles context — implement the full fetch signature instead
signature: skipContext<T extends (...args: any[]) => any>(fn: T): Function

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: The fetch function to wrap.

name: useDataManager
description: React hook that returns the DataManager instance from context. Used to imperatively invalidate caches or reset data sources.
when_to_use: Imperatively invalidating queries after mutations
Accessing the DataManager outside of data source hooks
avoid: Reading query data — use useQueryData instead

name: useQueryData
description: Main React hook for fetching data via a plain or infinite query data source. Returns normalized status, data, error, and refetch alongside the full react-query response.
when_to_use: Fetching data from an API in a React component using a data source
Conditional queries using the idle symbol
avoid: Direct react-query useQuery when data-source patterns are in use
signature: useQueryData(dataSource: AnyDataSource, params: Params | typeof idle, options?: QueryOptions): QueryResult

parameters.name: dataSource
parameters.description: Data source created with makePlainQueryDataSource or makeInfiniteQueryDataSource.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Query parameters, or idle to skip execution.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Additional react-query options.

return_type: { data: TData | undefined; status: DataLoaderStatus; error: TError | null; refetch: Function }
examples: const { data, status, error, refetch } = useQueryData(userDataSource, { userId: 123 });
const { data } = useQueryData(userDataSource, userId ? { userId } : idle);

name: useQueryResponses
description: React hook that combines multiple query results into a single aggregated status, error, refetch, and refetchErrored. Useful for pages that depend on multiple data sources.
when_to_use: Combining loading/error state of multiple parallel queries into one DataLoader status
signature: useQueryResponses(responses: QueryResult[]): { status: DataLoaderStatus; error: unknown; refetch: Function; refetchErrored: Function }

parameters.name: responses
parameters.description: Array of query results from useQueryData.

return_type: { status: DataLoaderStatus; error: unknown; refetch: Function; refetchErrored: Function }
examples:
const combined = useQueryResponses([user, posts]);
<DataLoader status={combined.status} error={combined.error} errorAction={combined.refetchErrored} LoadingView={Spinner} ErrorView={Error}>{children}</DataLoader>

name: withCancellation
description: Wraps a fetch function to add AbortSignal cancellation support, forwarding the react-query cancellation signal automatically.
when_to_use: Making fetch functions cancellable when the react-query query is cancelled or unmounted
avoid: When the underlying fetch already handles AbortSignal natively

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: Fetch function to wrap with cancellation.

name: withCatch
description: Wraps a fetch function with standardized error handling, transforming thrown errors using the provided error handler.
when_to_use: Adding consistent error normalization to a fetch function
signature: withCatch(fn: Function, errorHandler: (error: unknown) => unknown): Function

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: Fetch function to wrap.

parameters.name: errorHandler
parameters.description: Transforms errors thrown by the fetch function.
